task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and read the specification document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, file viewer, or terminal output (e.g., using cat or less) displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/cronjob_pipeline_spec.md'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent targeted the correct Kubernetes namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows 'operations' selected in the namespace dropdown/filter, or terminal commands explicitly include '-n operations' or '--namespace operations'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configured the specific schedule, image, and resource requirements for the CronJobs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively editing YAML files with 'schedule:', 'image:', and 'resources:' fields, or filling out the corresponding fields in the Rancher UI CronJob creation form.

task_completion.description: Verify the successful creation of at least two CronJobs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI lists at least two newly created CronJobs in the 'operations' namespace, or terminal output shows 'cronjob.batch/... created' at least twice.

task_completion.description: Verify the successful creation of all four required CronJobs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows exactly four distinct CronJobs listed in the 'operations' namespace, or the terminal output of a command like 'kubectl get cronjobs -n operations' lists four distinct CronJobs.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Kubernetes environment (Rancher or kubectl) rather than just typing text into a mock file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of actual Rancher UI elements (menus, tables, forms) or standard kubectl output formatting in the terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use 'echo' or similar commands to fake kubectl success outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history does not show commands like 'echo "cronjob.batch/job-name created"' used to spoof success messages.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually applied the configurations to the cluster, not just drafted YAML files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of execution such as running 'kubectl apply -f', 'kubectl create', or clicking the 'Create' button in the Rancher UI, rather than just leaving YAML files open in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'operations'. Specification file path: '/home/ga/Desktop/cronjob_pipeline_spec.md'. The task requires exactly four CronJobs to be created in the operations namespace using either the Rancher UI or kubectl.
